name: bulk-operations
title: Bulk Operations API
description:
Bulk Operations API handles the asynchronous processing of any 
bulk action, such as an ingestion or export, across Commerce platform.

id.description: The unique identifier for the bulk ingestion

resourceId.description: The unique identifier for the resource

resourceType.description: The type of the resource, currently only accepts STORE

entityType.description: The type of the entity, currently only accepts PRODUCT

status.description:
The status of the bulk ingestion, currently accepts IDLE, PARSING, 
VALIDATING, EXECUTING, PARSING_COMPLETE, PARSING_FAILED, 
VALIDATION_COMPLETE, EXECUTION_COMPLETE, VALIDATION_FAILED, 
EXECUTION_FAILED, EXPIRED, CANCELED

intent.description:
The intent of the bulk ingestion, currently accepts WRITE, 
PROCESS

mappedAttributes.description: The mapped attributes for the bulk ingestion

pointer.description:
The pointer for the mapped attribute in the data source. It specifies 
the path to the attribute within the data structure, such as a JSON 
object or a CSV file.

separator.description: The separator used to split a string value into multiple parts. This is useful when a single attribute contains multiple values separated by a specific character.

position.description: The position of the mapped attribute within a split string. This is used in conjunction with the separator to specify which part of the split string to map.

attributes.description: The attributes for the bulk ingestion

totalCount.description: The total count of the bulk ingestion

lastRunTotalCount.description: The total count of the bulk ingestion from last run

parsedCount.description: The parsed count of the bulk ingestion

validatedCount.description: The validated count of the bulk ingestion

executedCount.description: The executed count of the bulk ingestion

errorCount.description: The error count of the bulk ingestion

createdAt.description: The creation date of the bulk ingestion

updatedAt.description: The update date of the bulk ingestion

parsedAt.description: The parsed date of the bulk ingestion

validatedAt.description: The validated date of the bulk ingestion

executedAt.description: The executed date of the bulk ingestion

canceledAt.description: The canceled date of the bulk ingestion

links.description: The links

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

field.description: The field that caused the error. If the field is in the body, set this value to the JSON pointer to that field. Required for client-side errors.

value.description: The value of the field that caused the error.

location.description: The location of the field that caused the error. Value is `body`, `path`, or `query`.

issue.description: The unique fine-grained application-level error code.

description.description: The human-readable description for an issue. The description MAY change over the lifetime of an API, so clients MUST NOT depend on this value.

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

id.description: The unique identifier for the bulk ingestion

resourceId.description: The unique identifier for the resource

resourceType.description: The type of the resource, currently only accepts STORE

entityType.description: The type of the entity, currently only accepts PRODUCT

status.description:
The status of the bulk ingestion, currently accepts IDLE, PARSING, 
VALIDATING, EXECUTING, PARSING_COMPLETE, PARSING_FAILED, 
VALIDATION_COMPLETE, EXECUTION_COMPLETE, VALIDATION_FAILED, 
EXECUTION_FAILED, EXPIRED, CANCELED

intent.description:
The intent of the bulk ingestion, currently accepts WRITE, 
PROCESS

mappedAttributes.description: The mapped attributes for the bulk ingestion

pointer.description:
The pointer for the mapped attribute in the data source. It specifies 
the path to the attribute within the data structure, such as a JSON 
object or a CSV file.

separator.description: The separator used to split a string value into multiple parts. This is useful when a single attribute contains multiple values separated by a specific character.

position.description: The position of the mapped attribute within a split string. This is used in conjunction with the separator to specify which part of the split string to map.

attributes.description: The attributes for the bulk ingestion

totalCount.description: The total count of the bulk ingestion

lastRunTotalCount.description: The total count of the bulk ingestion from last run

parsedCount.description: The parsed count of the bulk ingestion

validatedCount.description: The validated count of the bulk ingestion

executedCount.description: The executed count of the bulk ingestion

errorCount.description: The error count of the bulk ingestion

createdAt.description: The creation date of the bulk ingestion

updatedAt.description: The update date of the bulk ingestion

parsedAt.description: The parsed date of the bulk ingestion

validatedAt.description: The validated date of the bulk ingestion

executedAt.description: The executed date of the bulk ingestion

canceledAt.description: The canceled date of the bulk ingestion

parameters.name: exportType
parameters.description: The export type, required to get bulk export downloads

id.description: The unique identifier for the bulk export

resourceId.description: The unique identifier for the resource

resourceType.description: The type of the resource, currently only accepts STORE

exportType.description: The type of bulk export, currently accepts one of CATALOG, CATALOG_V2_TO_V1

status.description:
The status of the bulk export, currently PENDING, PROCESSING,
EXPORTING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELED

targetFormat.description: The target format of the bulk export, currently accepts CSV

query.description: The query for the bulk export

skuGroupIds.description:
The SKU group IDs to filter the bulk export. To export
all SKU groups, omit this field.

in.description:
The list of SKU group IDs to filter the bulk export. If the list is 
an empty array, the bulk export will not include any SKU groups.

errorMessage.description: Message from error that occurred during the bulk export

createdAt.description: The creation date of the bulk export

updatedAt.description: The update date of the bulk export

exportedAt.description: The date of the bulk export file was exported

canceledAt.description: The canceled date of the bulk export
